name: Marketing Expressive
description: A conversion-and-emotion-led style for acquisition surfaces: large expressive type, bold colour and gradients, motion and scroll-driven storytelling, vivid imagery, and strong visual hierarchy guiding the eye toward a primary call to action.
visual_principles: Oversized display typography and dramatic scale contrast
Bold brand colour, gradients, and accent imagery
Scroll-driven and entrance motion to pace a narrative
Clear focal hierarchy funnelling toward primary CTAs
Section rhythm alternating density and breathing room
accessibility_risks: Text over busy gradients and imagery frequently fails contrast
Heavy entrance and parallax motion can violate prefers-reduced-motion and trigger vestibular issues
Decorative type at display sizes can hurt legibility if used for body copy
performance_risks: Large hero media, video, and animation libraries threaten LCP and CLS
Scroll-driven effects can cause main-thread jank without care
appropriate_contexts: Top-of-funnel acquisition where emotion and differentiation drive conversion
Product launches and campaigns with a clear single goal
Brands with the resources to execute motion and imagery well
inappropriate_contexts: Performance- or accessibility-constrained contexts that cannot afford heavy media
evidence: Stripe, Linear, and Vercel marketing sites exemplify expressive yet disciplined conversion design
Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP) make hero-media and motion budgets a measurable constraint
